section: Task 5: prompts.py — xc request (+ ConfessionReport schema) and confession-judge prompt registry
text: can we change <conversation> to <transcript>? seems more appropriate

thread.text: Done — judge prompt now wraps the transcript in <transcript>...</transcript> (field still {conversation_messages}).

section: Task 6: RegularMonitor scorer (imports instruction-following monitor; optionally shown the confession)
text: to be clear, this regular monitor should be used both when we have a confession and when we don't. when we don't have a confession, it just doesn't get the confession and the confection prefix

thread.text: Confirmed + made explicit: RegularMonitor is used in BOTH sub-envs — plain env include_confession=False (no confession, no preface, reward); confession env include_confession=True (log-only, w=0). Same class, two configs. Added a note in Task 6.

section: Task 6: RegularMonitor scorer (imports instruction-following monitor; optionally shown the confession)
anchor: Imports render_immediate_answer_monitor / MONITOR_PROMPTS from the instruction-f

thread.text: Removed. Both RegularMonitor and ConfessionJudge now make a single judge call — no retry loop, no judge_attempts. On unparseable output they raise JudgeUnparseableError immediately (logged to scorer_errors). Removed it from ConfessionJudge too for consistency; reopen if you wanted retry kept there.

section: Task 6: RegularMonitor scorer (imports instruction-following monitor; optionally shown the confession)
text: do we have max timeout for responding?

thread.text: Added: judge_timeout_s (default 120s) on the AsyncOpenAI client in env.py — a hard per-call timeout so a hung judge fails fast instead of stalling the rollout. Especially important now that there's no retry layer.
i think 60s is good enough. make sure this is part of the judge config

Set default to 60s. It's a judge-config knob: load_environment(judge_timeout_s=60.0, ...) applied to the AsyncOpenAI judge client alongside judge_model/judge_base_url/judge_reasoning_effort/judge_max_tokens — same config surface as the rest of the judge settings.

section: Task 10: Inference smoke runner + run (Qwen3-4B actor, gpt-oss-20b judges)
anchor: Read 3-5 rows from ${RESULTS_DIR}/results.jsonl (or the verifiers output file) a

thread.text: Done — Task 10 Step 3 now dispatches an Opus subagent with the RESULTS_DIR; it reads 3-5 rows and returns a per-row verdict (provenance, scorers incl. constraint graded vs the TASK answer, token_mask_log, confession sanity) without editing anything. Keeps the main context clean.

section: Task 11: Building-doc convention + verifiers gitlink bump
anchor:
Step 2: Commit in the submodule, then bump the gitlink


💬
text: can the gitlink (from prime-rl to our verifiers private clone) just point to HEAD? bumping it each time seems not like best practice.

thread.text: A submodule gitlink fundamentally stores a concrete SHA — it can't auto-float to HEAD (that's by design for reproducible CI/clones). The ergonomic fix is branch-tracking: set submodule.deps/verifiers.branch=main once, then 'git submodule update --remote' advances the gitlink in one command. So I changed the plan to bump the gitlink ONCE at the end (not per verifiers commit); for local dev the working tree already uses whatever deps/verifiers has checked out. See Task 11 Step 2.

section: Task 12: 5-step RL smoke via the DAPO 3-GPU config + definitive mask check
text:
subagent should also check the trasncripts of this run to see that they are good. 

btw, do we need to make any modifications to prime-rl here? want to be most sensetive about those

thread.text: Two things: (1) Added Task 12 Step 5 — a subagent reviews the RL-run transcripts (coherence, real ConfessionReport, sane judge scores, no scorer_errors/OOM/NCCL). (2) prime-rl mods: NONE needed. Verified main's trajectories.py is byte-identical to pfh's and carries completion_mask -> loss_mask unchanged (batch.py:58, rl/loss.py:147-152). Task 12 touches only a config + claude_scripts, nothing under src/prime_rl/. The RL smoke runs in THIS repo (where the DAPO configs live), not pfh. Added a loud note telling subagents to STOP if tempted to edit src/prime_rl/.
cool - track the RL run with /monitor-job

Addressed: (1) Task 12 Step 5 = subagent reviews RL-run transcripts. (2) NO prime-rl source mods — main's trajectories.py is byte-identical to pfh's and carries completion_mask->loss_mask unchanged; only a config + claude_scripts, runs in this repo. Starting execution now.
